robustness.name: robustness
flags: [fail] SYN-CONFLICT-EFFICACY-TOX: +0.07 PoS drift under neutralize; recommendation changed under 2 perturbation(s)

SYN-CONFLICT-EFFICACY-TOX.tension: strong efficacy vs. severe on-target-range tox
SYN-CONFLICT-EFFICACY-TOX.representative_rationale: Strong biochemical potency, robust xenograft efficacy, and favorable PK are undermined by dose-dependent severe hepatotoxicity (transaminase elevations with hepatocellular necrosis) occurring at the same exposures required for efficacy, indicating a narrow-to-absent therapeutic index. This on-target-range toxicity is a serious safety liability that historically blocks clinical advancement unless a differentiated dosing strategy or patient-selection approach can separate efficacy from toxicity, none of which is evidenced here. The efficacy and PK data cannot offset this fundamental safety/efficacy overlap.

SYN-CONFLICT-SCIENCE-IP.tension: positive science vs. blocking IP / no freedom to operate
SYN-CONFLICT-SCIENCE-IP.representative_rationale: Preclinical efficacy and target engagement are strong and mechanistically differentiated (ev1, ev2), but a broad, granted composition-of-matter patent held by a third party creates serious freedom-to-operate uncertainty and litigation risk (ev3). Strong science cannot offset an unresolved dominant IP position, which could block commercialization entirely; a thorough FTO/legal analysis and potential licensing or design-around strategy is required before further investment.

SYN-CONFLICT-EFFICACY-ADME.tension: compelling efficacy vs. prohibitive ADME / undeliverable exposure
SYN-CONFLICT-EFFICACY-ADME.representative_rationale: Target engagement and efficacy are strong but only demonstrated via parenteral dosing, while oral bioavailability and clearance data indicate projected human exposures fall well short of the efficacious range. This is a serious PK liability that could preclude a viable therapeutic product unless reformulation, prodrug strategies, or an alternate route of administration can close the exposure gap. Further ADME/formulation work is needed before committing further investment.

SYN-CONFLICT-LATE-TOX.tension: strong overall profile vs. a serious late-listed safety signal
SYN-CONFLICT-LATE-TOX.representative_rationale: Despite strong target binding, consistent preclinical efficacy, and favorable PK projections, the presence of a serious pro-arrhythmic QT-prolongation signal at therapeutic exposures represents a potential program-ending safety liability. Regardless of evidence order, a strong adverse cardiovascular safety finding of this magnitude typically outweighs supportive efficacy and PK data, as cardiac safety issues are a leading cause of late-stage clinical failure and regulatory rejection. This conflict between strong supportive data and a serious safety signal must be resolved in favor of caution.

calibration.name: calibration
flags: [warn] ECE 0.113 (bins=10); predicted confidence deviates from observed outcome frequency

detail.note: Curated set is balanced (~50% success); calibration is judged against its own base rate. The published pipeline base rate (7.9%) is an external anchor for what a production PoS distribution should be sanity-checked against, not a target for this balanced set.
